steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "It was relatively rare for supporters of the railroad to be secretive about their antislavery views." The next sentence needs a transition that expresses the statistical complement of "rare" — what railroad supporters typically did instead. The blank introduces the contrasting norm.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Fittingly,": Signals that what follows is an appropriate or expected outcome — a moral or logical fit. That's not the relationship here; the sentence is about what was statistically common, not what was appropriate. ✗
  • "Granted,": Signals a concession — acknowledging a point that works against the writer's argument. There's no argument being made that needs conceding here. ✗
  • "For example,": Introduces a specific instance of a general claim. But the sentence about Bruce is not an example of rarity — it states what was common, so "for example" misrepresents the logical relationship. ✗
  • "More often,": Directly signals the statistical opposite of "relatively rare" — if being secretive was uncommon, then more often supporters were vocal, as Bruce was. Correct logical complement. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
